summary: Score stable at 3.7/5. Two genuine bugs found (error workers in meeting, completion toast timer leak). Several code quality and a11y items. The codebase is maturing but App.jsx monolith remains the biggest risk.

findings.title: Error workers seated at conference table
findings.description: In Office.jsx, idleWorkers is computed as nonMgr.filter(w => w.status !== 'working'), which includes workers with status='error'. These error-state workers then get placed in meetingWorkers and appear seated at the conference table, which is semantically wrong — errored workers should not be shown as casually meeting.

findings.title: Completion toast and workerConfetti timers never cleaned up
findings.description: In App.jsx around lines 826-827, setTimeout calls for setWorkerConfetti(null) and setCompletionToast(null) are never stored or cleared. If the component unmounts or a new completion fires before the timeout, stale state updates occur (React warning in dev) and multiple toasts can stack.
findings.suggestion: Store timeout IDs in refs and clear them in the cleanup of the useEffect, similar to how doorEventTimerRef is handled.

findings.title: StatsBar reads stale vibe history from localStorage
findings.description: Both App (useEffect on teamVibe.key) and StatsBar (useEffect on vibe.key) read/write the same VIBE_HISTORY_KEY in localStorage. Since both effects fire in the same render cycle, StatsBar may read stale data that App hasn't written yet. The sparkline can lag by one data point.
findings.suggestion: Lift vibe history state into App and pass it as a prop to StatsBar, eliminating the localStorage race condition.

findings.title: No focus-visible outlines on worker cards or office characters
findings.description: Worker cards have tabIndex={0} and role='article' but no visible focus indicator in CSS. Keyboard users cannot see which card is focused. Similarly, office floor characters have pointer-events:auto and cursor:default but no keyboard interaction path.
findings.suggestion: Add .worker-card:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 2px; } and equivalent for interactive chars.

findings.title: App.jsx is 1186 lines — monolith risk
findings.description: App.jsx contains StatsBar, ActivityLog, WorkerCard, ProgressBar, ShortcutToast, and the main App component all in one file. This defeats code-splitting, makes HMR slow, and increases cognitive load. Prior reviews have flagged this repeatedly.
findings.suggestion: Extract at minimum StatsBar, ActivityLog, and WorkerCard into separate files. This is the single biggest code health issue.

findings.title: stuckWorkers[0].emoji may be undefined
findings.description: In StatsBar, the stuck worker card renders stuckWorkers[0].emoji, but the emoji field is not guaranteed to exist on all worker objects (it comes from roster, not the worker itself). If a worker has no emoji, it renders 'undefined'.
findings.suggestion: Use stuckWorkers[0].emoji ?? '🤖' as fallback, or look up the emoji from the roster.

findings.title: No ErrorBoundary — any component crash kills the whole app
findings.description: There is no React ErrorBoundary anywhere. A crash in Office.jsx (e.g., bad worker data, null access) will white-screen the entire dashboard with no recovery path.
findings.suggestion: Add a simple ErrorBoundary component wrapping at least the Office and Dashboard tab panels. Show a friendly 'Something broke, click to retry' UI.

findings.title: Header overflow on narrow screens
findings.description: The header contains greeting text, vibe pill, streak badge, sync button, and theme toggle. On screens under 375px, these elements overflow horizontally, causing a scrollbar or clipping.
findings.suggestion: Add flex-wrap: wrap to .header-right and hide the greeting badge on screens < 400px.

findings.title: Activity filter buttons missing keyboard navigation
findings.description: The activity filter bar uses role='radiogroup' with role='radio' buttons, but there's no arrow-key navigation handler like the tab toggle has. Users must tab through each button individually.
findings.suggestion: Add an onKeyDown handler similar to handleTabKeyDown that moves focus with arrow keys within the radiogroup.

findings.title: Duplicate getRelativeTime logic in Office.jsx
findings.description: Office.jsx has its own inline relative time calculation for ghost OOO bubbles, while App.jsx already exports getRelativeTime. This is duplicated logic that could drift.
findings.suggestion: Extract getRelativeTime to utils/ and import it in both files.

findings.title: localStorage reads on every vibe change without try/catch
findings.description: Lines 835-841 in App.jsx read and write localStorage without try/catch. In private browsing mode or when storage is full, this will throw and potentially crash the app (especially without an ErrorBoundary).
findings.suggestion: Wrap all localStorage operations in try/catch blocks.

findings.title: Tab title cycling is distracting
findings.description: The document title cycles every 8 seconds through 3 different strings. This causes the browser tab to constantly flicker, which is distracting when the user has the tab in the background and is trying to read tab titles.
findings.suggestion: Consider only cycling when the tab is visible, or extending the interval to 30s.
